name: Tajik Autonomous Soviet Socialist Republic (1929–1931)

colors.symbolism: the hammer and sickle and the state emblem
the wreath of wheat and cotton

description: A red field bearing the state emblem in the upper hoist: a gold hammer and a local sickle (dās) set against a five-pointed star above sun-rays rising over snowy mountains, encircled by sheaves of wheat and cotton.
history: Adopted in April 1929 for the Tajik Autonomous SSR — autonomous within the Uzbek SSR. It replaced the brief February 1929 first flag with a formalised state emblem, and bore the slogan 'Proletarians of all countries, unite!' and the republic's name in Russian and Tajik. Used until the fourth congress of soviets adopted a simpler lettered flag on 24 February 1931, by which time Tajikistan had become a full union republic.

features.role: state emblem of the Tajik ASSR
features.symbolism: a gold hammer and sickle on a star above sun-rays and mountains, within a wreath of wheat and cotton — Soviet worker-peasant unity with local agriculture and geography
